Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to create my applicant profile,
So that I can prepare for applying for available jobs.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can enter personal and academic information including name, student ID, programme, year of study, skills, and availability.
2. Required fields must be completed before saving.
3. The profile can be saved and reopened later.
4. An error message is shown if required fields are missing.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to edit my applicant profile,
So that my information remains accurate before or after submitting an application.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can update saved profile information.
2. Updated information is saved correctly.
3. Existing data is not lost unexpectedly.
Notes: Profile maintenance after first draft submission.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to upload my CV,
So that I can complete the application requirements for job applications.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can upload a CV file from local storage.
2. The uploaded file name is displayed after a successful upload.
3. The applicant can replace the uploaded CV with a new version.
4. An error message is shown if no file is selected.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to browse available jobs,
So that I can identify suitable opportunities before applying.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system displays a list of open jobs.
2. Each job card shows at least the title, module or activity, hours, deadline, and status.
3. Closed jobs are not shown in the default list.
Notes: Core browsing function in pre-application stage.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to filter and search jobs,
So that I can find suitable jobs more efficiently.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can filter jobs by module or activity, type, and keyword.
2. Filtered results update correctly.
3. An empty-state message is shown if no result matches.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to view detailed job information,
So that I can decide whether I should apply.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can open a job detail page from the job list.
2. Job details include description, requirements, hours, deadline, and organiser information.
3. The page clearly shows whether the job is open or closed.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to submit an application for a job,
So that I can be considered for the position.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant must complete profile information before applying.
2. The applicant must upload a CV before applying.
3. The system confirms successful submission.
4. The submitted application is recorded in the applicant's application list.
5. The MO can view the submitted application.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to view the status of my submitted applications,
So that I know whether my application is under review, waitlisted, rejected, or selected.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can open a 'My Applications' page.
2. Each application shows job title, application date, and current status.
3. Status labels are displayed clearly and consistently.
4. The applicant can access related job details from the application record.

Description:
As a TA applicant,
I want to withdraw my application before a decision is made,
So that I can stop being considered if my situation changes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can withdraw an application only when its status is still pending or under review.
2. Withdrawn applications are clearly marked.
3. The MO can see that the application was withdrawn.

Story Name: View Selection Result and Assignment Details
Description:
As a selected TA,
I want to see that I have been selected and view the related assignment details,
So that I know what role I have been assigned to.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The selected applicant can see the updated status as 'Selected'.
2. The assigned job details are clearly displayed.
3. The selected TA can view module or activity name, expected hours, and organiser information.

Description:
As a selected TA,
I want to confirm that I have seen my selection result,
So that the system can record my acknowledgement.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The selected TA can open the assigned job record and acknowledge the result.
2. The system records the acknowledgement status.
3. A confirmation message is shown after acknowledgement.

Description: As an MO, I want to create and publish a job post so that qualified students can apply for available TA opportunities.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The MO can enter job title, module or activity, role description, requirements, hours, deadline, and number of vacancies.
2. Required fields must be completed before publishing.
3. Published jobs appear in the TA applicant job list.
4. The MO can save the job as a draft or publish it directly.

Description: As an MO, I want to edit or close a job post so that outdated or filled jobs are no longer available for new applications.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The MO can edit job details before the deadline.
2. The MO can close a job post manually.
3. Closed jobs are marked as closed and cannot receive new applications.

Description: As an MO, I want to view applicants for a specific job so that I can review candidates efficiently.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The MO can open a list of applicants for each posted job.
2. Each applicant entry shows name, programme, availability, and application status.
3. The applicant count is displayed clearly for the job.

Description: As an MO, I want to review an applicant's full profile and CV so that I can make an informed selection decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The MO can open an applicant detail page from the applicant list.
2. The page shows profile information and CV access.
3. The MO can return to the applicant list without losing context.

Description: As an MO, I want to mark applicants as selected, rejected, or waitlisted so that the recruitment outcome is recorded clearly.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The MO can set the application outcome to Selected, Rejected, or Waitlisted.
2. The updated decision is reflected in the applicant list.
3. The TA applicant sees the updated status in My Applications.

Description: As an admin, I want to view each TA's overall workload so that I can monitor allocation fairness and identify over-allocation risks.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The admin can view a list of selected TAs with workload summary.
2. The summary includes TA name, number of assigned posts, and total hours.
3. Potentially overloaded TAs are easy to identify.

Description: As an admin, I want to filter workload records so that I can inspect specific TAs or overloaded cases more efficiently.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The admin can search by TA name or student ID.
2. The admin can filter by workload status.
3. An empty-state message is shown if no record matches the filter.

Description: As an admin, I want to see workload alerts for selected TAs so that I can quickly identify fairness or over-allocation issues.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system flags selected TAs whose workload exceeds a defined threshold.
2. Alerted records are visually distinguishable in the workload view.
3. The admin can inspect the related assignments for the flagged TA.
